title: SSE reconnect has no backoff — tight error loop floods UI and network
description: When the SSE stream ends (StreamEnded), the main loop sets sse_needs_restart=true and immediately re-spawns the SSE task at the top of the next loop iteration with zero delay. If the server is unreachable, this creates a tight reconnect loop: each failed subscribe_events() returns instantly with an Err, sends StreamEnded, and the main loop spins again. This floods the error display and saturates the network stack. The bootstrap phase has exponential backoff; the SSE reconnect path does not. A contributor looking at the SSE code in isolation will not see that the architecture needs a delay here.
